description_long: This object is a cluster of four irregularly shaped, faceted rocks with a reddish-brown coloration, resembling weathered sandstone or granite. The largest rock forms the central mass, with three smaller rocks grouped closely around its base, creating a natural, uneven formation. The surfaces are angular and polygonal, with visible flat planes and sharp edges, giving the rocks a stylized, low-poly appearance. Subtle variations in shading and highlights suggest a slightly matte, rough texture, typical of natural stone. The overall grouping appears solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings. This formation would serve as a naturalistic barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A cluster of four reddish-brown, faceted rocks with a rough, angular surface and a natural, solid appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the largest rock at the center, flanked by three smaller rocks, with the grouping appearing most balanced and prominent.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the height of the central rock and shows two smaller rocks in the foreground, with the third partially obscured.
description_view_2: From this angle, the rocks appear more spread out horizontally, with the central rock dominating the composition and the others forming a base.
description_view_3: The rear view highlights the back and top surfaces of the rocks, with the largest rock partially obscured by a smaller one in the foreground.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
